task.title: Setting preferences for COBOL code templates

titlealts.navtitle: Setting preferences for COBOL code templates
titlealts.searchtitle: Setting preferences for COBOL code templates

task.shortdesc: You can modify, add, or delete COBOL template code and comments.

keywords.indexterm: setting preferences for COBOL code templates

context.p: You can change the existing comment of the template or generated code, add comments or code to a template, use code or comments from an existing XML file, or save one or more features to an XML file. You can restore the product defaults for the templates. You can also enable bidirectional visual editing.

steps.stepsection: To configure generated COBOL code and comments, complete the following steps:

step.cmd: On the Preferences window, navigate to COBOL Features Code Templates .
step.stepresult:
A list of the currently configured COBOL templates for comments and code are displayed. The existing comment templates under Comments are as follows:
Copybook Comment
Program Comment
Data Division Comment
Environment Division Comment
ID Division Comment
Procedure Division Comment The existing code templates under Code are as follows:
Copybook
Program
Data Division
Environment Division
ID Division
Procedure Division

step.cmd: Perform one or more of the following tasks:

ol.li: Select a name under Comments or Code.
Click the Edit button. The Edit template window opens.
Edit the template, and click OK.
Click the Import button. The Open window opens.
Select an XML file, and click Open.
Click the Export All button, or select one or more features and click the Export button. The Export COBOL features window opens.
Specify the location and file name of the XML file to be created, and click Save.
Click the Restore Defaults button. The Confirm Restore Defaults window opens.
Click OK. All COBOL feature customizations are removed. The template defaults of a product are restored.

chrow.chdesc: Select the Enable BiDi Visual Editing checkbox.
